<zone>

Zone: Web Server API (`web-server-api`)
Files: 48, Cohesion: 0.87, Coupling: 0.13
Risk: healthy (score: 0.13)
Description: The Express-based server composition root for the web package, containing route handlers, MCP gateways, scheduler registration, usage tracking services, and the wiring that bridges the server tier to the viewer and domain layers.
Entry points: packages/web/src/server/shared-types.ts, packages/web/src/server/task-usage.ts

</imports>

<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.87) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.87 is strong for a 48-file composition root that must wire together gateways, schedulers, and usage services — this reflects disciplined separation of server concerns from viewer concerns.
[observation] [info] The injection seam (start.ts → register-scheduler.ts) bypasses static boundary analysis; any refactor of broadcast or loadPRD signatures must include a manual audit of RegisterSchedulerOptions to prevent silent interface drift.
[observation] [info] shared-types.ts and task-usage.ts are declared entry points, indicating these types and services are consumed by other zones; ensure they remain thin re-export or type-only modules to prevent the server's internal implementation from leaking outward.
[relationship] [info] Call graph (0 outgoing, 172 incoming) vs import graph (5 outbound imports into web-dashboard-viewer) discrepancy confirms the 5 server→viewer imports are type-only or build-time references generating no runtime calls. The server is a strict consumer of the viewer's public API, not a peer — the asymmetry is architecturally correct.
[anti-pattern] [warning] RegisterSchedulerOptions (packages/web/src/server/register-scheduler.ts lines 28, 31, 37) declares broadcast, collectAllIds, and loadPRD as optional, but the CLAUDE.md injection seam registry lists all four injected callbacks as always-provided by start.ts. Required callbacks marked optional silently accept undefined callers — future injection sites that omit a callback will fail at runtime rather than at compile time. Required callbacks should be non-optional in the interface.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.87) — files are tightly interconnected
- High cohesion (0.87) with low coupling (0.13) across 48 files (including tests) demonstrates a well-scoped server layer that correctly concentrates composition logic without over-importing from downstream zones.
- Five outbound imports into web-viewer and 84 inbound from web-viewer confirm the server is a consumer of the viewer's public API, not a peer — this asymmetry is architecturally correct for a composition root.
- The injection seam in start.ts → register-scheduler.ts (broadcast, collectAllIds, loadPRD, getAggregator) means four behavioral dependencies are invisible to static analysis; the RegisterSchedulerOptions interface must be kept in sync manually when either side changes.
- Cohesion of 0.87 is strong for a 48-file composition root that must wire together gateways, schedulers, and usage services — this reflects disciplined separation of server concerns from viewer concerns.
- The injection seam (start.ts → register-scheduler.ts) bypasses static boundary analysis; any refactor of broadcast or loadPRD signatures must include a manual audit of RegisterSchedulerOptions to prevent silent interface drift.
- shared-types.ts and task-usage.ts are declared entry points, indicating these types and services are consumed by other zones; ensure they remain thin re-export or type-only modules to prevent the server's internal implementation from leaking outward.
- The call graph shows 172 incoming calls into web-server-api with 0 outgoing, while the import graph records 5 imports from web-server into web-dashboard-viewer — this discrepancy indicates the 5 import edges are type-only or narrow utility imports that generate no runtime calls, confirming the server is a pure consumer of the viewer's public API with no callback into web-server from viewer at runtime
- Call graph (0 outgoing, 172 incoming) vs import graph (5 outbound imports into web-dashboard-viewer) discrepancy confirms the 5 server→viewer imports are type-only or build-time references generating no runtime calls. The server is a strict consumer of the viewer's public API, not a peer — the asymmetry is architecturally correct.
- RegisterSchedulerOptions declares broadcast, collectAllIds, and loadPRD as optional (?: syntax, lines 28, 31, 37) but start.ts always provides all three at the injection call site. The optional-but-always-provided pattern means a future caller that accidentally omits a callback will receive undefined at runtime rather than a compile-time error — the interface under-specifies the actual contract established by the injection seam registry in CLAUDE.md.
- RegisterSchedulerOptions (packages/web/src/server/register-scheduler.ts lines 28, 31, 37) declares broadcast, collectAllIds, and loadPRD as optional, but the CLAUDE.md injection seam registry lists all four injected callbacks as always-provided by start.ts. Required callbacks marked optional silently accept undefined callers — future injection sites that omit a callback will fail at runtime rather than at compile time. Required callbacks should be non-optional in the interface.
- [call graph] 1208 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 172 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
